item.title: Staff Post - Open
item.content:encoded: The image that's used in this article comes from here and is cc-compliant with the SCP wiki's license (CC BY-SA 3.0).

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
well yes .-. I wasn't suggesting linking a deleted scip, I just got a kick out of remembering that old article.

anyway, that's not really an objective reason to not implement expanded tags, that's an opinion, but not necessarily an invalid one. opinions can change, and I was just giving my opinion on the matter. The added convenience, especially to newcomers, outweighs this perceived hamfistedness, imo.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Heh, there's also that long-since-deleted scip

Deleted articles are pretty obviously not relevant to Wiki-Walk. There's no point in cross-linking to stuff that wasn't very good and no longer exists.

and I can't think of a negative reason for giving people more topics they can search for.

Accelerondo:

I want to deny spectral, on the basis that it's describing a quality of an anomaly that really isn't a physical attribute.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: Heh, there's also that long-since-deleted scip with the flowers that spawned fae-like entities that would die for no particular reason when they learned that their queen was no longer around. Anyway, there should be tags for "archetypes" of entities, like Fae, Werewolf, Ghost, Zombie, etc etc. A lot of people are fans of specific monster types and I can't think of a negative reason for giving people more topics they can search for.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
From the first days I was on the site, I always hoped that I would find some of these SCPs near where I live (based on where the image was taken and what's in the image), and my wish has now come true! The Angel Oak is right down the street from my house. Neato!

Otherwise, +1 because this was well-written and all that C:

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
This is why I think we really ought to extend tags to include these kind of categories, and I don't really understand the reasoning why we don't. It would be really nice if you could just search for all the skips with faeries or ghosts or whatever. Oh well.

+ The ones with faeries in them.
– hide block

SCP-001-O5
SCP-1323
SCP-2323
SCP-2523
SCP-2615 (and the related SCP-2615-J and SPC-2615)
SCP-2932
SCP-2952
Japanese Yokai:
SCP-2478
SCP-2863
SCP-2953
Project Osanshouo, 2006

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded: I can't say I'm familiar with "fae" lore, sorry. Could you link some?

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
This connection makes absolutely no sense. I don't see it as being even tangentially similar to Pitch Haven, unless you care to explain your reasoning beyond 'vibes'.

I think you'd get more milage out of something thematically related, perhaps relating to a fae article, or something specifically involving forgotten stories fading into obscurity. I've got nothing off the top of my head, unfortunately, but one could certainly find a more appropriate crosslink.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - WikiWalk Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Edit: community rejected proposal

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ show block
– hide block

Alright, I get some serious Pitch Haven vibes from this. All in all, this is already pretty similar to SCP-2988, which is not only a tree with weird body parts, but also a part of the Pitch Haven canon.

Editing this:

Only a few fairy tales can last

To this:

Only a few fairy tales can last

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think I would have liked it the way it was before, as it is now, I wouldn't like it with or without the addenda. It's just plain bad.

Downvoted.

item.title: Re: Da Rewrite is up
item.content:encoded: Sure thing, I'll replace it.

item.title: Re: Da Rewrite is up
item.content:encoded:
Minor quibble-

Instances of SCP-1580-1 display no anomalous properties until they are no longer attached to SCP-1580

Could be shortened to

Instances of SCP-1580-1 display no anomalous properties until they are detached from SCP-1580

I think. Seems clearer than before at any rate; easier for me to read.

item.title: Da Rewrite is up
item.content:encoded: Basically, switched it from a tree with its mind messed up from modern society to a tree trying to survive in modern society. Have at it!

item.title: Im'a rewrite dis shit
item.content:encoded: Thought about this for a while… And I think I'll rewrite this, eventually. I'm not happy with the addendum, the rating, tone in some parts. Wacky stuff is fun, but I'm going to listen to people's advice this time and make a more thought-provoking article.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The past tense of "shed" is also "shed".
As in, "SCP-1580 had recently shed a large number of leaves."

When I got to "poleafs" I expected to find another "back roots" argument about transcription and homophone puns that only work when written out. I am disappointed in the lack of lack of immaturity in this discussion thread. Y'all are no fun anymore!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I upvoted this but…

Fuck the poleafs

This made me laugh and not in a good way.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, I agree. I was ready to upvote this before the wildly off transcript. It really ruins the nice fairy-tale mood the earlier part builds up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The endless cyle of falling and reattaching leaves had a sort of creepy fairytale vibe to it, which I liked a lot; but the absurdist/slapstick nature of the addedendum has a completely different emotional impact, and the two impressions don't mix well at all. In that regard, I'm with Bright.

Also, while thing-that-does-a-thing has fallen out of favor, it's still a perfectly valid style of article if it's done well. I assume the reason it's discouraged is the same reason that humanoids etc. are discouraged for new members: because it's hard to do well, and easy to do poorly. Without the addendum this wouldn't have quite enough content but it would still have been most of the way to an interesting, well written TTDAT.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understand where Lee is coming from. Upvoted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Leaves that reattach themselves comes off as boring! The log adds so much more character and story. Without it there isn't a hook as it just becomes "leaves that reattach themselves" or a thing that does a thing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I absolutely love the originality of this. Well done!
This is the kind of charm I look for in an SCP.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think the log adds some personality to the SCP, I mean look at those leaves, everyone of them is like a person living in a community (which is the tree). All of them have their own personality and their own place in 1580.

This +1 comes because the SCP is a great idea, it's well written, I like it and because the log made me lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All good points. I'll remove it for now, but will edit it for later.

(Though I was thinking the tree didn't pass on its traits to its offspring, but I should've thought about that)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I felt the log added to the SCP . Saying to the reader that the foundation not only contains evil malevolent entities but also the mundane seemingly harmless. The known existence of any sort of magical or abnormal my cause the public to question "things" more. Making the foundation's work be more difficult to carry out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I have some issues with it:

  • It doesn't really feel like an Object Class thing. I think if I had to include this information in the article, I would drop it in the Special Containment Procedures. It just feels more relevant there.
  • "Instances Secured" and "Fully Contained" are also redundant. If all instances are secure, there's no way for it to be not fully contained. If it's fully contained, that implies that none of the instances remain unsecured.
  • Living things have a tendency to pass on traits to their offspring. If the cause of this is genetic (or epigenetic), then this is not fully contained. If there's straight up just another somewhere, it's not fully contained. They don't know this stuff yet. Which means that they should have a full sentence explaining that, not a few words in the Object Class that sound more authoritative than they really are.
  • They don't do enough to distinguish it from other articles, because probably more than half of the things on the site are fully contained.
  • From a narrative standpoint, it's only interesting when the thing isn't contained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Important-ish reply: After waiting a bit, I'm glad to see the way I wrote this got the right reaction. That is, by being mysterious and then slapping the reader with weird stuff. Of course not everyone would like it; it totally and utterly breaks the mood of it. Just goes to show how anomalies aren't always what you expect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't think the log ruins it, but I will say there's quite the detachment between the whimsicality of the article and the comedy of the log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Your vote is justifiable, but I would like to ask; what do you find wrong with the details in the object class?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Safe; Fully Contained, Instances Secured.

I have an issue with this. And well, I second Bright (that log was not satisfactory). -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I liked it a lot more until the log. Then downvoted. It works perfectly fine as leaves that reattach themselves… but loses a lot with the 'talk like regular people in traffic'

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is weird and simple and I like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are some XXXX's left near the bottom of the transcript.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Thanks to those who looked at this in chat!

The image source… It's all over the Internet. But I think it's from here? I can't really verify it.

And sorry Jelly Roll, I have no regrets

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Not for me it doesn't. Weird.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It works. Actually, it worked before you replied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks once again.

But hold up while I try to make the image work. >:c

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There was one XXXX left. But now it's gone c:
